title: Living in Boulder vlog 2 - 6  - 2017: Google Earthing Theater of Castrense

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a captain's log update on his Google Earth project to map and calculate seating capacities of ancient Roman venues, including Circus Maximus, Stadium of Domitian, Theater of Marcellus, and smaller theaters. He explains his methodology of tracing stands at 19-inch intervals and discusses plans to rebuild the Theater of Castrense in Google Earth to visualize individual people inside ancient Roman seating structures. He briefly mentions waiting on results from a cancer biopsy.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Rome would be the perfect city to host a massive gathering of YouTubers or performers — it has enough seating capacity to hold a million people across its venues
Skeptical of the 260,000 figure; his own calculations came out to 150,000
Worth rebuilding as a small-scale model to calibrate understanding of what people looked like inside Roman stands

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to figure out the seating capacity of all the different venues in Rome.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rome was the perfect vacation city and a perfect place for people to get together.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: I calculated the seating capacity of Circus Maximus to be 47,000 people initially.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: After adding stands behind the columns, the seating capacity of Circus Maximus increased to 150,000.

claims.subject: Wikipedia
claims.claim: The Wikipedia article estimates the seating capacity of Circus Maximus at 260,000.

claims.subject: Stadium of Domitian
claims.claim: The Stadium of Domitian has 26 kilometers worth of stands, which is more than the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Stadium of Domitian
claims.claim: The seating capacity of the Stadium of Domitian is estimated at 52,000 people.

claims.subject: Wikipedia
claims.claim: The Wikipedia article states the seating capacity of the Stadium of Domitian is 15,000 to 20,000.

claims.subject: Theater of Marcellus
claims.claim: The Theater of Marcellus has a seating capacity of 17,000.

claims.subject: Circus Domitian
claims.claim: I estimated the seating capacity of Circus Domitian to be about 30,000.

claims.subject: Theater of Caustic Strip
claims.claim: I wanted to rebuild the Theater of Caustic Strip, which is a smaller version of a Caustian theater.

claims.subject: Theater of Caustic Strip
claims.claim: The Theater of Caustic Strip has a seating capacity of 355 people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I am currently tracing the seating arrangement for the Theater of Caustic Strip to visualize the people inside.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I am listening to different decades of music while working on the seating arrangements.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I received a cancer cutout today and will find out in five days if it is cancer.
